zh.Old Basing, Hampshire, England: 英國哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Porter於大約1173年出生在英國哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛時，他的父親Adam Porter23歲，母親Mabilla Aureval d'Orival21歲。他和Godeheut Paynel至少有5個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1213年在英國哈普爾波斯特享年41歲。

zh-hans.Old Basing, Hampshire, England: 英国哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Porter于大约1173年出生在英国哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛时，他的父亲Adam Porter23岁，母亲Mabilla Aureval d'Orival21岁。他和Godeheut Paynel至少有5个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1213年在英国哈普爾波斯特享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Porter was born about 1173, in Old Basing, Hampshire, England, his father, Adam Porter, was 23 and his mother, Mabilla Aureval d'Orival, was 21. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Godeheut Paynel. He died in 1213, in Portchester, Hampshire, England, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Porter est né environ 1173, à Old Basing, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Adam Porter, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Mabilla Aureval d'Orival, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Godeheut Paynel. Il est décédé en 1213, à Portchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Porter um 1173 in Old Basing, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam Porter 23 und seine Mutter Mabilla Aureval d'Orival 21. Er hatte mit Godeheut Paynel mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1213 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Portchester, Hampshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Porter è nato il circa 1173 nel Old Basing, Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Adam Porter, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Mabilla Aureval d'Orival, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con Godeheut Paynel. Morì nel 1213, nel Portchester, Hampshire, England, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.Old Basing, Hampshire, England: イギリスハンプシュアイアオルド バシング
ja.Portchester Castle, Hampshire, England: イギリスハンプシュアイアポルトチェスター
ja.Portchester, Hampshire, England: イギリスハンプシュアイアポルトチェスター
ja.lifeSummary: William Porterが約1173年にイギリスハンプシュアイアオルド バシングで生まれた時，父Adam Porterは23歳，母Mabilla Aureval d'Orivalは21歳であった。彼はGodeheut Paynelとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1213年にイギリスハンプシュアイアポルトチェスターで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Porter(이)가 대략 1173년 영국 함프실르 드 바징에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam Porter(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabilla Aureval d'Orival(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Godeheut Paynel(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1213년 영국 함프실르 포트체스터에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Porter nasceu aproximadamente 1173, em Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam Porter, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Mabilla Aureval d'Orival, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas com Godeheut Paynel. Ele faleceu em 1213, em Portchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Porter родился около 1173 г. в местности олд басинг, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Adam Porter было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Mabilla Aureval d'Orival, 21. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от Godeheut Paynel. Он умер в 1213 г. в местности порчестир, хампшир, Англия в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Porter nació aproximadamente 1173, en Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam Porter, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Mabilla Aureval d'Orival, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas con Godeheut Paynel. Murió en 1213, en Portchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años.
